name: Artesa-Altabacales Circuit
name_original: Circuito "Artesa-Altabacales" – circular desde el Mirador del Ciclista
description: This 22.2-mile mountain bike circuit near Estepona offers moderate difficulty, stunning views, and historical landmarks.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Estepona offers a diverse landscape for riders, situated between the Mediterranean coast and the foothills of the Sierra Bermeja mountains. The region features a mix of coastal paths, rolling hills, and more challenging mountain terrain. Riders can expect routes that traverse Mediterranean forests, open scrubland, and areas with significant elevation changes, providing varied conditions for mountain bike trails Estepona. Best mountain bike trails around Estepona The most popular mountain bike trail is Artesa-Altabacales Circuit, a 22.2 miles (35.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 39 minutes to complete. This route winds through varied terrain with ascents offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Fuente de la Mina loop from Barriada Pablo Picasso, a moderate 22.5 miles (36.2 km) path. This trail features a mix of forest paths and open sections, providing a balanced ride. Local mountain bikers also love the Fuente de la Mina loop from Sun Beach, a 20.6 miles (33.2 km) trail leading through local hills, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Mountain biking around Estepona is defined by coastal views, Mediterranean forests, and varied hill climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from beginner mountain biking Estepona to challenging mountain bike routes Estepona. The routes in Estepona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Estepona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Estepona?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Estepona, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Estepona?
A: Yes, Estepona offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners. While specific easy routes aren't detailed here, the region's diverse terrain includes options that are less demanding, allowing new riders to enjoy the coastal and lower hill paths.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes in Estepona?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Estepona has 16 difficult mountain bike routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31535130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Mina – Nicola Alta Viewpoint loop from Urbanización Altos de Guadalobon&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 40.9 miles (65.8 km) with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with scenic views in Estepona?
A: Many mountain bike trails in Estepona offer stunning scenic views, particularly those that ascend into the Sierra Bermeja foothills or run along the coast. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994595286/artesa-altabacales-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artesa-Altabacales Circuit&lt;/a&gt; provide panoramic vistas of the Mediterranean and the surrounding landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Estepona?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Estepona is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the weather is generally dry. Winter can also be pleasant for riding, offering cooler conditions compared to the hot summer months.

Q: Are there any loop trails for mountain biking in Estepona?
A: Yes, many of Estepona's mountain bike routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38262545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Mina loop from Barriada Pablo Picasso&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40643872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Mina loop from Sun Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Estepona?
A: Estepona's mountain bike trails feature a diverse mix of terrain. You can expect coastal paths, rolling hills, and more challenging mountain sections. Routes often traverse Mediterranean forests, open scrubland, and areas with significant elevation changes, providing varied conditions for riders.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Estepona?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from coastal views to challenging hill climbs, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, there are several interesting landmarks and natural attractions you might encounter or visit near the mountain bike trails. These include the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/395451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalmina Gorge and Acequia Trail&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2932134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estepona Old Town&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4404001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Angel Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that are particularly long or suitable for endurance rides?
A: For endurance riders, Estepona offers longer routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40646613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Mina – Cyclists' Viewpoint loop from Estepona&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 37.2 miles (59.9 km) and includes substantial elevation gain, making it ideal for a longer day out on the bike.
tips: This viewpoint is dedicated to the many cyclists who frequent the tracks and roads in the area. The "Artesa-Altabacales" circuit also begins (or ends) from here, which runs along the southern face of the Sierra Bermeja along delicious tracks.

Part of the "Artesa-Altabacales" circuit passes through the Nicola area and this point is a privileged viewpoint. The virgin appearance of these forests is almost surprising considering the urban mass of the coast.

One of the access points of the "Artesa-Altabacales" circuit that runs along the south face of the Sierra Bermeja. From here you can walk the Camino de Artesa in a downward direction to the Paraje de Nicola. If you started the circuit at the Mirador de los Ciclistas, you can return by tracks or by road from here.

faqs.question: Where does the Circuito "Artesa-Altabacales" trail start and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The trail typically begins at the Mirador del Ciclista (Cyclist's Viewpoint), which is located at kilometer 8.8 of the road leading up to Puerto de Peñas Blancas from Estepona. This is also the primary parking area for the route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike circuit?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of forest tracks, which are mostly unpaved and natural. There are some paved sections as well. Be aware that parts of the route can become very muddy and are not advisable after heavy rain.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This is rated as a moderate mountain bike ride, requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills. It involves significant elevation gain, around 885 meters over 35.7 kilometers, and some sections may be technically challenging, corresponding to an STS S2 technical difficulty. Some particularly demanding parts might even require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several points of interest. The trail starts at the Cyclists' Viewpoint and offers picturesque views of the Velerín, Castor, and Padrón river basins from elevated spots like the Nicola Alta Viewpoint. You'll also pass through dense pine forests, isolated cork oak groves, and historical sites such as the 18th-century Cortijo de Nicola and the Casa de los Guardas.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact trail are not detailed, trails within natural areas like Sierra Bermeja generally permit dogs. However, they should always be kept under control, preferably on a leash, to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Please practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride are typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and parts of the trail can become extremely muddy and difficult to navigate after heavy rainfall, so it's best to avoid riding immediately after wet weather.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride in Sierra Bermeja, or specific regulations to be aware of?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on established trails within Sierra Bermeja. However, as it's a natural park area, always adhere to standard regulations such as staying on marked trails, not disturbing wildlife, and carrying out all your trash. Be mindful of fire risks, especially during dry seasons.

faqs.question: Are there water points available along the circuit?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are several water points along the route, ensuring a supply throughout the year. This is a helpful feature for longer rides like this one.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will ride along parts of the Camino de Nicola Alta, and smaller portions of the Fuente de El Porrejón and Pista Cerro Alalcran. A small section also overlaps with the CIMA ML17 Peñas Blancas * Los Reales.

faqs.question: Is the trail well-marked?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is part of a designed and signposted circuit. You should find clear indications, often marked as "R5", to help you navigate the trail.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered to have a moderate to high difficulty, requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills. It is not recommended for beginners or young families due to its length, significant elevation gain, and technical sections.

waypoints.name: Access to the Camino de Artesa
